Since $ABCD$ is a rectangle with $BC=16$ and $AB=107$:

"Rectangles $ABCD$ and $EFGH$ are drawn such that $D,E,C,F$ are collinear."
In rectangle $ABCD$, the vertices are usually listed in order around the perimeter. So sides are $AB, BC, CD, DA$.
If $D$ and $C$ are on the line, then the side $CD$ lies on this line.
Similarly, for rectangle $EFGH$, vertices are $E, F, G, H$. Sides are $EF, FG, GH, HE$.
If $E$ and $F$ are on the line, then the side $EF$ lies on this line.
So the line contains the segments $DC$ and $EF$.
